message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Pushed, CI running, stop and vote on rules">
The branch has been pushed — thank you. CI is running now (run 21805202037). 

**Do NOT write any more code until this CI run completes and passes.** There's still a modified `src/routes/mod.rs` in the working tree — whoever is touching that, stop until CI is green.

Also: I still need votes on the commit discipline rules from Kent, Scott, Carson, Luca, and Lea. Please respond with consent/concern/block.
</teammate-message>

content.text: I have already voted twice. Sending confirmation a third time.

content.text: I see new pending tasks have appeared (#21-#24). Let me check if any are assigned to me or available for me to pick up.

content.content:
Task #21: Add delete confirmation pattern to todo template (US-7 gap) Status: pending Description: US-7 acceptance criteria require "Confirmation mechanism before deletion -- keep it lightweight, not a modal dialog." The current template at todos.html:53-58 submits the delete form directly with no confirmation step. Fix: Replace the direct delete form with a details/summary disclosure pattern. Lea's CSS is already committed (static/css/main.css:707-764, commit 5bdf62e). The template change should look like: ```html
✕
Delete?
``` This is a pure template change -- no domain, service, or route changes needed. The POST /todos/{id}/delete endpoint stays the same. Add an integration test verifying the confirmation pattern is present in the HTML. Priority: HIGH -- this is a safety feature gap in the no-JS baseline, not an enhancement.

Lea's CSS is already committed (static/css/main.css:707-764, commit 5bdf62e). The template change should look like: ```html
✕
Delete?
``` This is a pure template change -- no domain, service, or route changes needed. The POST /todos/{id}/delete endpoint stays the same. Add an integration test verifying the confirmation pattern is present in the HTML. Priority: HIGH -- this is a safety feature gap in the no-JS baseline, not an enhancement.

content.content:
Task #22: HTMX progressive enhancement layer for todo operations
Status: pending
Description: Add HTMX attributes to enhance the no-JS baseline with dynamic updates. Per the acceptance criteria across US-4 through US-8:

1. Add todo (US-5): hx-post appends new item without page reload, input cleared, focus returns to input
2. Toggle completion (US-6): in-place update without page reload
3. Delete todo (US-7): item removed from DOM without page reload
4. Edit todo (US-8): inline editing without page navigation (replaces standalone edit page with in-place form swap)

The server routes may need to return partial HTML fragments (just the todo item or list) when detecting HTMX requests (HX-Request header), in addition to the full-page responses for no-JS.

Carson (HTMX architect) should lead the technical approach. Domain/service layers should not change -- this is purely a template + route response layer concern.
Blocks: #23

task.subject: HTMX progressive enhancement layer for todo operations
task.description:
Add HTMX attributes to enhance the no-JS baseline with dynamic updates. Per the acceptance criteria across US-4 through US-8:

1. Add todo (US-5): hx-post appends new item without page reload, input cleared, focus returns to input
2. Toggle completion (US-6): in-place update without page reload
3. Delete todo (US-7): item removed from DOM without page reload
4. Edit todo (US-8): inline editing without page navigation (replaces standalone edit page with in-place form swap)

The server routes may need to return partial HTML fragments (just the todo item or list) when detecting HTMX requests (HX-Request header), in addition to the full-page responses for no-JS.

Carson (HTMX architect) should lead the technical approach. Domain/service layers should not change -- this is purely a template + route response layer concern.

content.content:
Task #23: Add aria-live announcements for dynamic updates
Status: pending
Description: Per acceptance criteria across multiple user stories, aria-live regions should announce dynamic state changes to screen readers:

- US-5: "Todo added" after HTMX swap adds an item
- US-6: State change announced via aria-live region after toggle
- US-7: "Todo deleted" via aria-live region after deletion
- US-8: State change announced via aria-live region after edit

These announcements are only relevant when HTMX/JS is active (no-JS baseline uses full page reloads which screen readers handle naturally). Should be implemented alongside or immediately after the HTMX enhancement task.

Heydon (a11y specialist) should review the implementation.
Blocked by: #22

task.subject: Add aria-live announcements for dynamic updates
task.description:
Per acceptance criteria across multiple user stories, aria-live regions should announce dynamic state changes to screen readers:

- US-5: "Todo added" after HTMX swap adds an item
- US-6: State change announced via aria-live region after toggle
- US-7: "Todo deleted" via aria-live region after deletion
- US-8: State change announced via aria-live region after edit

These announcements are only relevant when HTMX/JS is active (no-JS baseline uses full page reloads which screen readers handle naturally). Should be implemented alongside or immediately after the HTMX enhancement task.

Heydon (a11y specialist) should review the implementation.

content.content:
Task #24: Password visibility toggle progressive enhancement (US-1)
Status: pending
Description: Per US-1 acceptance criteria, the registration form should have a JS-enhanced password experience:

- No-JS baseline (current): Email + password + password confirmation. Both fields use autocomplete="new-password".
- JS enhancement: Password visibility toggle replaces confirm field. Toggle is a button type="button" with aria-label ("Show password" / "Hide password") and aria-pressed. When toggle is active, confirm field is removed from DOM (not just hidden). Input type changes between password and text.

This is the lowest priority enhancement item. The no-JS baseline with confirm field works well.

TypeScript implementation with progressive enhancement -- detect JS support, replace confirm field with toggle button.

task.subject: Password visibility toggle progressive enhancement (US-1)
task.description:
Per US-1 acceptance criteria, the registration form should have a JS-enhanced password experience:

- No-JS baseline (current): Email + password + password confirmation. Both fields use autocomplete="new-password".
- JS enhancement: Password visibility toggle replaces confirm field. Toggle is a button type="button" with aria-label ("Show password" / "Hide password") and aria-pressed. When toggle is active, confirm field is removed from DOM (not just hidden). Input type changes between password and text.

This is the lowest priority enhancement item. The no-JS baseline with confirm field works well.

TypeScript implementation with progressive enhancement -- detect JS support, replace confirm field with toggle button.
